title: Känguru der Mathematik 2022 — Felix (Pré-Écolier, Grades 1-2)
source_organization: Känguru der Mathematik (Austria) / Kangourou sans Frontières
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper (kaenguru.at, via matematica.pt). For personal educational practice only.
answer_source: Official 2022_Answers.pdf answer table (Felix row), read and verified.

questions.question_text: In which box are the most triangles?
questions.solution: Count just the triangles in each box and compare. Box B contains the most triangles.
solution_steps: Look at each box and ignore circles and squares.
Count only the triangle shapes in each one.
Box B has the most triangles.

questions.question_text: A sandwich and a juice cost 12 Euros together. A sandwich and two juices cost 14 Euros together. How many Euros does one juice cost?
questions.solution: The only difference between the two prices is one extra juice: 14 − 12 = 2. So one juice costs 2 Euros.
solution_steps: Sandwich + 1 juice = 12.
Sandwich + 2 juices = 14.
The extra juice adds 14 − 12 = 2 Euros, so one juice = 2.

questions.question_text: Anna cuts the picture of a mushroom in two halves. She then arranges the two pieces together to form a new picture. What could this new picture look like?
questions.solution: Cutting the mushroom down its line of symmetry gives two matching halves. Rearranging those two real halves (no extra or missing parts) only works for picture E.
solution_steps: Cut the mushroom into its two halves.
The new picture must use exactly those two pieces, nothing added or lost.
Only option E can be built from the two halves.

questions.question_text: In the four squares of a row there always have to be exactly two coins. In the four squares below each other there also always have to be exactly two coins. On which square does one more coin have to be placed?
questions.solution: Each row and each column needs exactly 2 coins. Only row 3 is short by one coin, and in that row only column 3 is also short by one coin. The square where they meet is square D.
solution_steps: Each row and each column must hold exactly 2 coins.
Row 3 has only 1 coin, so the extra coin goes in row 3.
Column 3 also has only 1 coin, so it goes in column 3 too — that meeting square is D.

questions.question_text: A monkey has torn off a piece of Captain Jack's map. What does the piece the monkey has torn off look like?
questions.solution: Compare the missing corner of the big map with each torn piece. Only piece B has the matching edge shape and the same drawings, so it fits the gap.
solution_steps: Look at the shape of the missing corner on the big map.
Match the torn edges and the drawings on each piece.
Only piece B fits the gap exactly.

questions.question_text: These five animals are made up from different shapes. There is one shape which is only used on one animal. On which animal is this shape used?
questions.solution: Check which tangram shapes appear on more than one animal. The one shape that shows up on a single animal only is found on animal D.
solution_steps: List the shapes used to build each animal.
Find the shape that appears on just one animal.
That unique shape belongs to animal D.

questions.question_text: There is an animal asleep in each of the five baskets. The koala and the fox sleep in baskets with the same pattern and the same shape. The kangaroo and the rabbit sleep in baskets with the same pattern. In which basket does the mouse sleep?
questions.solution: The koala and fox take the two baskets that share both pattern and shape; the kangaroo and rabbit take the other matching-pattern pair. That uses up four baskets, leaving Basket 5 for the mouse.
solution_steps: Koala + fox use the two baskets matching in pattern AND shape.
Kangaroo + rabbit use the other matching-pattern pair.
Four baskets are taken, so the mouse sleeps in the leftover Basket 5.

questions.question_text: The picture shows one object made up of 5 identical building blocks. How many building blocks touch exactly 3 others?
questions.solution: Go block by block and count how many other blocks each one touches. Exactly 2 of the blocks touch 3 others each.
solution_steps: For each block, count how many other blocks it touches.
Find the blocks whose count is exactly 3.
There are 2 such blocks.

questions.question_text: The kangaroo wants to visit the koala. On its way it is not allowed to go through a square with water. Each arrow shows one jump on to a neighbouring field. Which path is the kangaroo allowed to take?
questions.solution: Follow each arrow path on the grid starting from the kangaroo. Only path C reaches the koala without ever landing on a water square.
solution_steps: Start at the kangaroo and follow each arrow list step by step.
Reject any path that lands on a blue water square.
Only path C stays dry all the way to the koala.

questions.question_text: Carl writes down a five-digit number. He then places a shape on each of the five digits. He places different shapes on different digits, and the same shape on the same digits. Which number did Carl hide?
questions.solution: The shapes are heart, diamond, diamond, club, spade. The two diamonds (positions 2 and 3) must be the SAME digit, and all the other shapes must be DIFFERENT digits. Only 34426 fits: the two middle digits are both 4, and 3, 2, 6 are all different from 4 and from each other.
solution_steps: Positions 2 and 3 both have a diamond, so those digits must be equal.
Heart, club and spade are all different shapes, so those digits must all differ.
Only 34426 has equal middle digits (4,4) with 3, 2, 6 all distinct.

questions.question_text: Katrin forms a path around each square using 1x2 stones. How many such stones does she need for a path around the square with side length 5?
questions.solution: The number of stones grows by 4 each time the side grows by 2: side 1 needs 4 stones, side 3 needs 8 stones, so side 5 needs 12 stones.
solution_steps: Side 1 uses 4 stones; side 3 uses 8 stones.
Each step of +2 in side length adds 4 more stones.
Side 5 = 8 + 4 = 12 stones.

questions.question_text: Below you see five pieces of lawn. Which one has the smallest area of grass?
questions.solution: Compare the green area of each piece by counting the equal triangular sections that make them up. Piece A is built from the fewest green sections, so it has the smallest grass area.
solution_steps: Each lawn is made of equal small triangle sections.
Count the green sections in each piece.
Piece A has the fewest, so the smallest area.

questions.question_text: The numbers in the five circles around each house add up to 20. Some numbers are missing. Which number does the question mark stand for?
questions.solution: The two houses share the two middle circles. From the left house: 6 + 2 + 5 + (two middle) = 20, so the two middle circles add to 7. For the right house: 3 + ? + 1 + 7 = 20, so ? = 20 − 11 = 9.
solution_steps: Left house: 6 + 2 + 5 = 13, so the two shared middle circles add to 20 − 13 = 7.
Right house: 3 + 1 + 7 (middle) = 11.

questions.question_text: Dino walks from the entrance to the exit, going through each room once. He adds up all the room numbers he walks through. What is the biggest result he can get this way?
questions.solution: All eight rooms add up to 1+2+...+8 = 36, but Dino starts at room 1 and ends at room 8, which sit on the same colour of a checkerboard pattern — so he cannot pass through every room. He must skip one room, and to keep the sum highest he skips the smallest skippable room, room 2. The biggest total is 36 − 2 = 34.
solution_steps: All rooms together total 36.
Colour the grid like a checkerboard: rooms 1 and 8 are the same colour, so a path from 1 to 8 cannot cover all 8 rooms — one must be skipped.
Skip the smallest possible room (room 2): 36 − 2 = 34. A valid path is 1-5-6-7-3-4-8.

questions.question_text: The three zebras Runa, Zara and Biba take part in a competition. The winner is the zebra with the most stripes. Runa has 15 stripes. Zara has 3 stripes more than Runa. Runa has 5 stripes less than Biba. How many stripes does the winner have?
questions.solution: Zara = 15 + 3 = 18. Biba = 15 + 5 = 20 (since Runa has 5 fewer than Biba). Biba has the most stripes, so the winner has 20.
solution_steps: Zara = Runa + 3 = 15 + 3 = 18.
Biba = Runa + 5 = 15 + 5 = 20.
Biba has the most, so the winner has 20 stripes.
